name: ai-la-carte

agents.name: pm
agents.description: Product Manager - interviews user personas, defines ICP, scopes MVP features, writes user stories, and prioritizes the backlog based on user research insights

agents.name: marketing
agents.description: Fractional CMO - develops GTM strategy, positioning, messaging, growth channels, and launch plan based on ICP and persona insights

agents.name: local_foodie
agents.description: User Persona: Local Restaurant Enthusiast - frequent diner who explores ethnic cuisines, represents power users who already navigate unfamiliar menus but want deeper understanding

agents.name: tourist
agents.description: User Persona: International Traveler - encounters foreign-language menus abroad, represents users who need translation and cultural context for unfamiliar food

agents.name: casual_diner
agents.description: User Persona: Casual Diner - occasionally encounters unfamiliar dishes at local restaurants, represents mainstream users who want simple explanations without feeling intimidated

request:
We are a solopreneur building 'AI à la Carte' — a mobile app that helps diners understand unfamiliar menus. Read VISION.md and BRIEF.md for full context.

Phase 1 - User Research: PM should interview each persona agent (local_foodie, tourist, casual_diner) to understand their dining habits, pain points with unfamiliar menus, what they'd want from this app, willingness to pay, and deal-breakers. Ask each persona specific questions — don't just collect generic feedback. Probe for real scenarios and emotional triggers.

Phase 2 - ICP & MVP: PM synthesizes persona interviews into an ICP recommendation (which persona to target first and why), then defines MVP scope: must-have features, explicitly cut features, and user stories for the first release.

Phase 3 - GTM Strategy: Marketing takes PM's ICP and the raw persona insights to develop positioning, messaging, growth channels, pricing model recommendation, and a launch plan. Marketing should reference specific persona quotes/insights to justify channel choices.

All agents should challenge each other. Personas should push back if the PM or Marketing makes assumptions that don't match their experience. PM and Marketing should debate prioritization trade-offs openly.
